metadata.summary: A terminal UI for interacting with Github

documentation:
![PyPI - Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/lazy-github) ![PyPI - Downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dw/lazy-github)

LazyGithub is a terminal UI client for interacting with [GitHub](https://github.com). It draws heavy inspiration from the
[lazygit](https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazygit) project and uses [Textual](https://textual.textualize.io/) to drive the terminal UI interactions.

![Example screenshot](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/gizmo385/lazy-github/main/images/lazy-github-conversation-ui.svg)

## How to Use It

You can run the [most recently built version](https://pypi.org/project/lazy-github/) by installing it from PyPI. If you have [uv installed](https://github.com/astral-sh/uv), you can do that easily with `uvx lazy-github`.

When you first start LazyGithub, you will be prompted with a device login code and a link to GitHub
where you will be able to authenticate the app against your account. This allows the app to act on
your behalf and is necessary for LazyGithub to function.

Currently, it supports the following:

- Listing the repositories associated with your account 
- Listing the issues, pull requests, and actions on your repositories
- Listing the details, diff, and reviews on any of those pull requests
- Detailed issue and pull request views, including conversation participation

If you wish to run it from a local clone of the repository, you can do so by running the `./start.sh` located in the root of the repo.

## Customization

LazyGithub supports a number of customization options, all of which are stored in `$HOME/.config/lazy-github/config.json`.
These can be edited manually via changing the config or by opening the settings management UI within LazyGithub. That UI
can be accessed via the command pallete (`CMD+p`) and then searching for settings.

![Settings screenshot](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/gizmo385/lazy-github/main/images/lazy-github-settings-ui.png)

functions.name: lazy_github.lib.utils.pluralize
documentation:
Helper function for correctly pluralizing strings in the UI. This is simple but gets messy when written many times
across the UI code.

functions.name: lazy_github.lib.utils.bold
documentation: Wraps the given text in Rich bold tags

functions.name: lazy_github.lib.utils.link
documentation: Formats a link in Rich-style markup

classes.name: lazy_github.lib.utils.classproperty
documentation: Simple implementation of the @property decorator but for classes

functions.name: lazy_github.lib.github_cli._run_gh_cli_command
documentation: Simple wrapper around running a Github CLI command

functions.name: lazy_github.lib.github_cli.is_logged_in
documentation: Checks to see if the user is currently logged into the GitHub CLI

functions.name: lazy_github.lib.github_cli.fetch_notifications
documentation: Fetches notifications on GitHub. If all=True, then previously read notifications will also be returned

functions.name: lazy_github.lib.github_cli.unread_notification_count
documentation: Returns the number of currently unread notifications on GitHub

functions.name: lazy_github.lib.git_cli.current_local_repo_full_name
documentation: Returns the owner/name associated with the remote of the git repo in the current working directory.

functions.name: lazy_github.lib.git_cli.current_local_branch_name
documentation: Returns the name of the current branch for the git repo in the current working directory.

functions.name: lazy_github.lib.constants.favorite_string
documentation: Helper function to return the right string to indicate if something is favorited

functions.name: lazy_github.lib.constants.private_string
documentation: Helper function to return the right string to indicate if something is private

functions.name: lazy_github.lib.context.github_headers
documentation: Helper function to build headers for Github API requests

classes.name: lazy_github.lib.context.LazyGithubContext

methods.name: lazy_github.lib.context.LazyGithubContext.current_directory_repo
documentation: The owner/name of the repo associated with the current working directory (if one exists)
Globally accessible wrapper class that centralizes access to the configuration and the Github API client

classes.name: lazy_github.models.github.PartialPullRequest
documentation: A pull request that may be included in the response to a list issues API call and is missing some information

classes.name: lazy_github.models.github.FullPullRequest
documentation: More comprehensive details on a pull request from the API

classes.name: lazy_github.lib.logging.LazyGithubLogFormatter
documentation: A universal logging format that we can use

functions.name: lazy_github.lib.github.branches.list_branches
documentation: List branches on the specified repo

methods.name: lazy_github.lib.bindings.LazyGithubBindings.all
documentation: Returns all bindings which can be rebound

methods.name: lazy_github.lib.bindings.LazyGithubBindings.all_by_id
documentation: Returns a dictionary of all bindings which can be rebound, with the key being their ID

classes.name: lazy_github.lib.messages.RepoSelected
documentation:
A message indicating that a particular user repo has been selected.

This message is used to trigger follow-up contextual actions based on the selected repo, such as loading pull
requests, issues, actions, etc.

classes.name: lazy_github.lib.messages.PullRequestSelected
documentation: A message indicating that the user is looking for additional information on a particular pull request.

classes.name: lazy_github.lib.messages.IssueSelected
documentation: A message indicating that the user is looking for additional information on a particular issue.

classes.name: lazy_github.lib.messages.IssuesAndPullRequestsFetched
documentation:
Since issues and pull requests are both represented on the Github API as issues, we want to pull issues once and
then send that message to both sections of the UI.

methods.name: lazy_github.lib.github.client.GithubClient.github_headers
documentation: Helper function to build a request with specific headers

methods.name: lazy_github.lib.github.client.GithubClient.user
documentation: Returns the authed user for this client

functions.name: lazy_github.lib.github.issues.list_issues
documentation: Fetch issues (included pull requests) from the repo matching the state/owner filters

functions.name: lazy_github.lib.github.repositories._list_for_page
documentation: Retrieves a single page of Github repos matching the specified criteria

functions.name: lazy_github.lib.github.auth.get_device_code
documentation:
Authenticates this device with the Github API. This will require the user to go enter the provided device code on
the Github UI to authenticate the LazyGithub app.

functions.name: lazy_github.lib.github.auth.get_access_token
documentation: Given a device code, retrieves the oauth access token that can be used to send requests to the GIthub API

functions.name: lazy_github.lib.github.auth.save_access_token
documentation: Writes the returned access token to the config location

functions.name: lazy_github.lib.github.auth.token
documentation:
Helper function which loads the token from the file on disk. If the file does not exist, it raises a
GithubAuthenticationRequired exception that the caller should handle by triggering the auth flow

functions.name: lazy_github.lib.github.workflows.list_workflows
documentation: Lists available Github action workflows on the specified repo

functions.name: lazy_github.lib.github.workflows.list_workflow_runs
documentation: Lists github workflows runs on the specified repo

functions.name: lazy_github.lib.github.workflows.create_dispatch_event
documentation:
Creates a workflow dispatch event for the specified workflow. For properly configured workflows, this will trigger
a new one against the specified branch

functions.name: lazy_github.lib.github.pull_requests.list_for_repo
documentation: Lists the pull requests associated with the specified repo

functions.name: lazy_github.lib.github.pull_requests.get_full_pull_request
documentation: Converts a partial pull request into a full pull request

functions.name: lazy_github.lib.github.pull_requests.get_diff
documentation: Fetches the raw diff for an individual pull request

functions.name: lazy_github.lib.github.pull_requests.reconstruct_review_conversation_hierarchy
documentation:
Given a list of PR reviews, this rebuilds a the comment hierarchy as a tree of connected comment nodes. The return
value of this function is a mapping between the comment IDs and the associated ReviewCommentNode for the top level
comments ONLY. Any subsequent comments will be included as children in one of the review comment nodes.

An important disclaimer is that this function does NOT take into account the body associated with the review itself,
which is present in some reviews. When generating UI from this function, the body of review itself should be
included prior to printing the review comments themselves.

Given a variable `hierarchy` generated from a list `reviews` of PR reviews, the output of this can be properly
unpacked like so:
```python
for review in reviews:
    if review.body:
        # Output the root review body
        print(review.body)

        # Output the review comments that are top level (i.e. their ids are in the hierarchy map)
        for comment in review.comments:
            if comment.id in hierarchy:
                # Call
                comment_review_node_handler(hierarchy[comment.id])
```

functions.name: lazy_github.cli.cli
documentation: A Terminal UI for interacting with Github

functions.name: lazy_github.cli.run
documentation: Run LazyGithub

functions.name: lazy_github.cli.dump_config

documentation: Reset the user's settings

classes.name: lazy_github.lib.config.AppearanceSettings
documentation: Settings focused on altering the appearance of LazyGithub, including hiding or showing different sections.

classes.name: lazy_github.lib.config.NotificationSettings
documentation: Controls the settings for the optional notification feature, which relies on the standard GitHub CLI.

classes.name: lazy_github.lib.config.BindingsSettings
documentation: Custom keybinding overrides for LazyGithub. When rebinding, pressing ESCAPE will reset to the default binding.

classes.name: lazy_github.lib.config.RepositorySettings
documentation: Repository-specific settings

classes.name: lazy_github.lib.config.PullRequestSettings
documentation: Changes how pull requests are retrieved from the Github API

classes.name: lazy_github.lib.config.IssueSettings
documentation: Changes how issues are retrieved from the Github API

classes.name: lazy_github.lib.config.CacheSettings
documentation: Settings that control how long data will be cached from the GitHub API

classes.name: lazy_github.lib.config.ApiConfig
documentation: Controlling how the GitHub API is accessed in LazyGithub

documentation: Removes all rows currently displayed and tracked in this table

documentation: Override the set of rows contained in this table. This will remove any existing rows

documentation: Change which rows are currently displayed in the table
